description.value:
A bundled wad of dried marsh-root wrapped in oilcloth, smelling of peat and iron. When activated, the roots uncurl and release a warm, earthy vapour that clears the lungs and steadies the hands. Travellers in the Frostfen have used these for generations — the marshes poison you, but they also provide the cure.

Effect: As a maneuver, you can apply the poultice to yourself or an adjacent ally. The target immediately ends the [[/apply slowed]]{slowed} condition and gains the following benefits until the end of their next turn:

  • Cold immunity equal to 3 + the target's level.

  • An edge on Might tests to resist environmental cold, disease, or poison effects.

Additionally, if the target is suffering from marsh-sickness (the persistent cough from a failed Frostfen montage test), the poultice cures it immediately — the rattling clears and the bloodshot eyes fade.
description.director:
Found: Beat 4 — Montage Test 1. Can be discovered during a successful medium Intuition test to scout the Frostfen, or purchased from a refugee herbalist on the Herdweg for 1 day's rations (Heat currency). Also found in the medicine box at the Dead Waystation (Beat 8), though by then the family who owned it is beyond help.

Yield Note: An herbalist PC can craft these with appropriate Reason/Intuition tests during respites if they have access to marsh-root (common in the Frostfen, rare elsewhere).

project.prerequisites: Fresh marsh-root from the Frostfen, clean linen, a warm fire to dry the roots
project.source: Frostfen herbalist tradition (oral, no written texts)

effects.name: Marsh-Root Poultice (Cold Resistance)
effects.description: Cold immunity equal to 3 + your level and an edge on Might tests against environmental cold, disease, or poison until the end of your next turn.
